note: Fullcalendarview_generator is a submodule of fullcalendar_view providing exactly one Drush command that scaffolds a calendar View. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded) probing documented facts about the command's name, arguments, options and behavior; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: Which module gives me a Drush command to auto-generate a Full Calendar View, and what is the command name and its alias?
evals.expected_output: The fullcalendarview_generator submodule provides the Drush command fullcalendarview:generate, alias fcvg.

evals.prompt: What inputs does the fullcalendarview:generate (fcvg) command require to build a calendar View?
evals.expected_output: A view_name, a comma-separated list of content types (content_types_str), a start_date_field, and a page path; prompted interactively if omitted.

evals.prompt: How do I set an end-date field and enable the generated View when running fcvg non-interactively?
evals.expected_output: Pass --end_date_field=<field> for an optional end date and --enable to enable the View on creation (also --title_field, defaulting to title).

evals.prompt: What must be installed for the fullcalendarview_generator Drush command to work?
evals.expected_output: The submodule depends on views and fullcalendar_view; the command errors out if fullcalendarview_generator itself is not enabled.

evals.prompt: Can I run the fcvg calendar-view generator without passing any arguments?
evals.expected_output: Yes; run `drush fcvg` with no arguments and it prompts interactively (choice/multiselect/confirm) for view name, content types, fields, path, and enable.

evals.prompt: What does the fcvg command validate before it creates the calendar View?
evals.expected_output: It validates that the named content types exist, that the start/end/title fields exist on those types, and that the target path is not already routed/in use.
